name: Saucey Black Beans
recipeIngredient: 1 tablespoon grated peel from one orange
1/2 can mild green chilis (or whole can for chili lovers)
2 cans stewed or canned tomatoes (16 oz each)
4 cloves minced garlic (or 1 whole bulb of roasted garlic, peeled and chopped)
recipeInstructions:
1. Drain the soaking water from the beans and add the 12 cups water. Bring to a boil.
2. Reduce heat and cook at a fast simmer for 1 to 1 1/2 hours until the beans are just starting to soften.
3. Add all other ingredients except the molido, stirring occasionally, until the beans are truly soft.
4. Stir in the molido and turn off the burner. Allow to sit for 30 minutes.
5. Serve over rice or cornbread, or use in enchiladas.
6. Adjust cooking time according to taste, as beans may behave differently with each batch. If they seem too liquidy after adding onions, leave the lid off until they cook down.
NOTE: Some prefer using 2 tablespoons molido, but this is optional and depends on personal preference.
